title: anthocercis viscosa subsp. viscosa
intro: Anthocercis viscosa subsp. viscosa, also known as sticky honeysuckle, is a flowering plant native to Western Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a treatment for various skin conditions, such as eczema and dermatitis, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its health benefits, including wound healing and antimicrobial activity. The leaves and stems of the sticky honeysuckle can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and topical creams, making it a valuable resource in traditional and modern herbal medicine.
